ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: CELINDA ALIGADA, CSR NO. 13724, EMAIL: CELINDA_ALIGADA@HOTMAIL.COM ; TEL: 1-800-788-2021 (302/RBU)

Defendant New York Life Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Jun-13-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. FARAH TABIBKHOEI, Esq. of Law Offices of Scott C. Glovsky, APC (Tel: 626-243-5598) for plaintiff;
Atty. VANESSA R. CORNEJO, Esq. of La Follette, Johhnson, DeHaas, Fesler & Ames (Tel: 916-563-3100) for defendant Robert Johnson, M.D.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Venue.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's ("NY Life") motion to change venue to Stanislaus County is denied.

On February 15, 2023, the court issued an order stating: "NY Life's motion to change venue based upon the convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice is denied." (2:22-23.) NY Life points to no portion of that order or citation to the reporter's transcript from the oral argument where the court denied the motion "without prejudice." Therefore, NY Life is required to comply with the prerequisites of CCP 1008(b) for this renewed motion. NY Life fails to meet its burden. First, the motion does not technically comply with the declaration requirement of CCP 1008(b) ["it shall be shown by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown."]. Second, and more importantly, NY Life fails to demonstrate new facts or diligence. (Even Zohar Construction & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 839 ["Courts have construed section 1008 to require a party filing an application for reconsideration or a renewed application to show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not having presented the new or different information earlier."].) Here, NY Life contends that plaintiff recently served supplemental discovery responses that identified witnesses that had knowledge of the events. But NY Life knew or should have known of these witnesses before. NY Life presumptively had plaintiff's medical records and knew who the key players were before filing this action. (1/9/23 Halton Decl., Exs. A, C.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Celinda Aligada, CSR No. 13724, Email: celinda_aligada@hotmail.com ; Tel: 1-800-788-2021 (302/RBU)
